title: Review: Compiler trait conformance refactoring (23 suggestions from task 193)
analysis:
## Triage of 23 Suggestions Against Current Codebase

The 23 suggestions from esc-193-42 were produced by the task-193 steward review. However, task 193's code was subsequently reverted by commit 3a248e07 ('chore: add review follow-up tasks #384-#385'), which removed ~26k lines including:
- Public API functions: check_trait_conformance (public form), check_trait_conformance_chain, check_trait_conformance_multi
- Private helper: collect_chain_requirements
- ConformanceError enum, PortInfo/SubInfo types
- qualified_access_compile_tests.rs, annotation_compile_tests.rs, and other test files
- DefaultKind::Let struct variant (reverted to tuple variant)
- CompiledTrait.annotations field

### Applicable Suggestions (7 of 23)

1. **S3 (excessive arity)** — collect_all_requirements takes 10 mut ref params. Extract into RequirementCollectionState struct.
2. **S4 (semantic gap)** — structure_members HashMap<String, Type> conflates param and let. Currently the conformance check treats RequirementKind::Param and RequirementKind::Let identically, so this is by-design. Document the decision; add a targeted test.
3. **S7 (O(n*m) sub lookups)** — Sub-component lookup in check_trait_conformance iterates structure.members with .any() for each Sub requirement. Build a lookup map.
4. **S13 (test helper duplication)** — compile_module/compile_first_template duplicated across 12+ test files. Extract to tests/common/mod.rs.
5. **S21 (missing Sub dedup)** — RequirementKind::Sub requirements skip the seen_names dedup check in collect_all_requirements (lines 4528-4550). Two non-diamond traits with identical sub requirements produce duplicate errors.
6. **S22 (unbounded recursion)** — collect_all_requirements has no depth limit; only a visited set for cycle detection. Pathologically deep (but acyclic) chains could overflow the stack.
7. **S23 (asymmetric error filtering)** — Document that TypeMismatch errors are intentionally never suppressed by defaults (the structure explicitly provided a wrong type).

### Not Applicable (16 of 23)

S1, S2, S5, S6, S8, S9, S10, S11, S12 reference public API functions (check_trait_conformance_chain/multi, collect_chain_requirements, ConformanceError) that were reverted.
S14, S15, S16, S17, S18, S19 reference test files (qualified_access_compile_tests.rs, qualified_access_eval.rs) that were reverted.
S20 references port conformance checking (RequirementKind::Port) that doesn't exist in current code.

These will be re-assessed when qualified access (task 193) is re-implemented via tasks #384/#385.

### Approach

Prerequisites first: extract shared test helpers (S13) and create the state struct (S3). Then TDD steps for bug fixes (S21, S22) and performance (S7). Documentation changes (S4, S23) are inline with implementation.

prerequisites.description: Create crates/reify-compiler/tests/common/mod.rs with shared compile_module and compile_first_template helpers extracted from trait_conformance_tests.rs
Migrate trait_conformance_tests.rs to use `mod common;` instead of inline helper definitions. Verify all existing tests still pass.

Migrate remaining 11 test files (port_compile_tests, silent_defaults_tests, purpose_compile_tests, connect_compile_tests, field_compile_tests, occurrence_compile_tests, trait_bounds_tests, entity_overload_tests, meta_compile_tests, chained_comparison_tests, prepass_consolidation_tests) to use `mod common;`. Verify all tests pass.

steps.description: Write failing test: diamond_sub_requirement_deduped — two non-diamond traits A and B both declare `sub hole = Hole`, structure provides one hole. Assert exactly 0 errors (currently expect duplicate 'missing sub' errors if structure doesn't provide sub, or duplicate requirements collected). (S21)

Fix Sub dedup in collect_all_requirements: add a seen_sub_names HashSet<(String, String)> keyed by (name, structure_name). Skip duplicate Sub requirements. Report conflicting Sub requirements (same name, different structure_name) as diagnostics. (S21)

Write failing test: conflicting_sub_requirements — trait A declares `sub hole = Hole`, trait B declares `sub hole = Plug`. Structure implements both. Assert conflict diagnostic mentioning 'hole'. (S21 extension)
Implement Sub conflict detection: when seen_sub_names already has (name, different_structure_name), emit 'conflicting sub-component requirements' diagnostic. (S21 extension)

Write test: deep_chain_recursion_depth_limit — create a trait hierarchy 200 levels deep (programmatically generate source). Assert compilation doesn't panic/stack-overflow and produces a diagnostic. (S22)

Add depth parameter to collect_all_requirements (or use a counter in the state struct). When depth exceeds MAX_TRAIT_DEPTH (128), emit 'trait refinement chain too deep' diagnostic and return early. (S22)
Write test: sub_conformance_basic — trait declares `sub hole = Hole`, structure provides matching sub. Assert no errors. This tests the linear-lookup path that will be optimized. (S7 + coverage gap)

Refactor check_trait_conformance: before the requirement loop, build a HashMap<&str, &str> for sub-component lookups (name -> structure_name) from structure.members. Replace the O(n) .any() iteration with O(1) HashMap lookup. (S7)

Write test: param_vs_let_cross_satisfaction — trait requires `param x : Length`, structure has `let x : Length = 5mm`. Assert NO error (documenting that param-vs-let is intentionally fungible in conformance checking). (S4 documentation test)

Refactor collect_all_requirements signature: extract 7 mutable parameters (requirements, defaults, visited, seen_names, seen_defaults, seen_sub_names, depth) into a RequirementCollectionState struct. Pass &mut state instead. Add doc comments explaining param-vs-let fungibility (S4) and TypeMismatch non-suppression (S23). (S3 + S4 + S23)

design_decisions.decision: 16 of 23 suggestions triaged as NOT APPLICABLE because they reference code reverted by commit 3a248e07
design_decisions.rationale: Task 193's public API (check_trait_conformance_chain/multi, ConformanceError, collect_chain_requirements) and associated test files were removed. These suggestions should be re-evaluated when qualified access is re-implemented via tasks #384/#385.

design_decisions.decision: Param-vs-let fungibility in conformance checking is intentional (S4) — document, don't change
design_decisions.rationale: The current code treats RequirementKind::Param and RequirementKind::Let identically when checking structure_members. Both resolve through the same HashMap<String, Type>. The trait system's purpose is to ensure structural completeness (the right named members with the right types exist), not to enforce param-vs-let semantics. Adding param/let distinction would break existing code where structures use `let` to satisfy a `param` requirement. Add a test documenting this as intentional behavior.

design_decisions.decision: Extract test helpers to common/mod.rs rather than a separate crate
design_decisions.rationale: Rust integration test convention uses `tests/common/mod.rs` with `mod common;` in each test file. This avoids adding a new crate dependency and follows idiomatic Rust test organization. The 12+ files with duplicated compile_module/compile_first_template all have identical bodies.

Realistic trait hierarchies rarely exceed 10 levels. 128 provides ample headroom while preventing stack overflow from pathological inputs. Each recursion frame is modest (~200 bytes for refs), so 128 levels < 25KB stack usage.

design_decisions.decision: State struct extraction (S3) done LAST to avoid merge conflicts
design_decisions.rationale: The state struct refactor touches the same function signature as S21 (sub dedup) and S22 (depth limit). Doing it last means all functional changes are in place and the refactor is purely structural, minimizing risk.

design_decisions.decision: guard_compilation.rs excluded from common/mod.rs migration — it only has compile_first_template, not compile_module
design_decisions.rationale: guard_compilation.rs defines compile_first_template inline (which internally calls a local compile_module), so it can still benefit from the common module. Include it in pre-3 migration.

reuse.how: Extract to tests/common/mod.rs verbatim; all 12+ copies have identical bodies

reuse.what: Existing seen_names HashMap<String, Type> dedup pattern for Param/Let
reuse.how: Apply same pattern for Sub requirements using HashSet<(String, String)> keyed by (name, structure_name)

reuse.what: Existing visited HashSet cycle detection pattern
reuse.how: Add depth counter alongside visited set; same early-return pattern with diagnostic emission
New diagnostics for conflicting subs and depth limit follow the established pattern: Diagnostic::error(format!(...)).with_label(DiagnosticLabel::new(span, label_text))
